title: make 9p security model configurable; document
body:
#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?
Yes, but defaults are unchanged.

```release-note
Support specification of the 9p security model for `podman machine` volumes ([#13784](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13784)).
```

Please see this bug for background information:
https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13784

Thanks,
Corey

comments.body:
> please squash all commits into one! Thanks for the contribution.

Ok; done. Thanks for the review.

Does it make sense to do this per volume or should this be a system wide setting.  IE Should we just add the security-model flag to containers.conf and then have podman machine just read it and apply it to all volumes?

> Does it make sense to do this per volume or should this be a system wide setting. IE Should we just add the security-model flag to containers.conf and then have podman machine just read it and apply it to all volumes?

The different security models allow (and prevent) different use cases.
* `none` allows for my own use case of providing the VM (hence containers) access to a git repo working copy which has symlinks, but it's not possible to operate on files in ways that need root access.
* `mapped-xattr` emulates root access, thus allowing for a use of a service (such as a database) which needs persist storage of files which have ownership within the VM (hence container) different from the user running the VM. Unfortunately, this also breaks sharing of symlinks by design (I could only speculate as to why).

I'm probably oversimplifying the differences a bit; this is just what I've found from experimentation.

Anyway, I wouldn't want to prevent anybody from simultaneously running containers with different use cases, so the setting should be per-volume.
